title: Open WebUI
oneLiner: A self-hosted AI interface for local and remote models.
summary: Open WebUI is a self-hosted AI interface for local and remote models with a familiar chat experience and an active open-source ecosystem.
whyItMatters: Open models need usable interfaces. Open WebUI gives teams and individual builders a practical way to run model workflows without starting from a blank app shell.
notFor: Teams that need a fully managed SaaS-only product
seoTitle: Open WebUI: Open-source self-hosted AI interface
seoDescription: A concise editorial profile of Open WebUI, including what it does, who it is best for, license, repo, and related open-source AI resources.
shareDescription: A self-hosted AI interface for local and remote models.
featuredReason: A practical interface layer for builders running local and open AI stacks.

coreStrengths.title: Self-hosted AI workspace
coreStrengths.description: Open WebUI gives teams and individual builders a chat-style interface they can run themselves for local and remote models.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: A usable interface is often the missing layer between open models and daily work.

coreStrengths.title: Local model workflow support
coreStrengths.description: It is especially relevant for users building around local model stacks such as Ollama and OpenAI-compatible endpoints.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: This lets teams experiment with open or local models without building a full app shell.

coreStrengths.title: Operational control
coreStrengths.description: Because it is self-hosted, teams can evaluate deployment, data, and model routing under their own constraints.
coreStrengths.whyItMatters: That is useful when a managed SaaS interface is not acceptable for privacy, customization, or infrastructure reasons.

useCaseNotes.title: Internal AI chat workspace
useCaseNotes.description: Use it as a self-hosted place for teams to interact with approved local or remote models.

useCaseNotes.title: Ollama-powered local AI
useCaseNotes.description: A natural fit for users who want a friendlier interface on top of local model workflows.

useCaseNotes.title: Model experimentation UI
useCaseNotes.description: Useful when comparing models, prompts, and workflows without building a custom frontend first.

compareNotes.title: Choose it when self-hosting matters
compareNotes.summary: Managed tools are easier to start with, but Open WebUI is stronger when control, local models, or customization matter.

compareNotes.title: Plan for operations
compareNotes.summary: Self-hosting gives control, but it also means you own deployment, upgrades, and access management.

thumbnailBrief.visualMotif: clean utility panel with model selector and chat surface
